skill.name: building-rag-systems
skill.description: Build production RAG systems with semantic chunking, incremental indexing, filtered retrieval, Qdrant payload indexes, and context expansion patterns.
skill.summary: Build production-ready RAG ingestion and retrieval pipelines with Qdrant and OpenAI embeddings.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, C2, certificate, and weak-crypto alerts are false positives caused by Markdown fences, diagrams, proficiency labels, and SHA-256 guidance. The skill is not malicious, but its production API examples omit path allowlisting, authentication, and webhook signature implementation details, so publication should include a security warning.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Document Path in Ingestion Example
medium_findings.description: The ingestion API passes request.docs_path into a background ingestion job. If copied into production without allowlisting or path normalization, it could read unintended local documents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data flow is visible in the example, but the referenced run_ingestion_job implementation is not included. This is a guidance risk, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Webhook Verification Left as Placeholder
medium_findings.description: The GitHub webhook example says to verify the signature but leaves the implementation as pass. Users could copy an unauthenticated trigger endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly marks signature verification as a TODO-style comment. The risk depends on whether users implement the missing control.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick patterns occur in Markdown code fences, inline command text, diagrams, and Python snippets. The skill only includes documentation plus a verification script, and no backtick execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is Markdown formatting or sample code after reviewing the files. No executable Ruby or shell backtick call exists in the skill files.

low_findings.title: API Key Alert Is Legitimate Client Configuration
low_findings.description: The Qdrant API key reference is used to configure a Qdrant client in sample code. No evidence found that the key is logged, transmitted to an unrelated endpoint, or embedded as a literal secret.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is clearly a client constructor for Qdrant. It is still sensitive configuration, but there is no exfiltration pattern.

low_findings.title: C2 Keyword Alert Is a Proficiency Level
low_findings.description: The C2 token appears as a language proficiency enum value beside A1 through C1. It is not command-and-control terminology in this context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding Literal enum contains education proficiency levels. The semantic context rules out C2 infrastructure intent.

low_findings.title: Certificate Key File Alerts Are Set Operations
low_findings.description: The certificate or key-file detections point to Python set comparisons between indexed and current file paths. No certificate, private key, or key material is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines create Python sets from path collections. The static keyword match does not align with the actual code meaning.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: No weak hash algorithm is present at the cited frontmatter location. The actual hashing guidance uses SHA-256 for change detection, not for password storage or signing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is descriptive metadata, and the reviewed hashing example uses SHA-256. No MD5, SHA-1, or unsafe crypto use was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Match RAG Traversal Logic
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance-like terms describe document crawling, Qdrant scrolling, and prev/next chunk traversal for retrieval. These are expected RAG implementation patterns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text explains indexing and context expansion behavior. No host reconnaissance, process enumeration, or network discovery is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copy-Paste Production API Needs Input Controls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill presents API snippets as production patterns. The examples should add path allowlisting, authentication, authorization, and webhook signature verification before deployment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The snippets are incomplete examples rather than executable package code. The missing controls are still important because the skill labels the patterns as production guidance.

content.value_statement: Production RAG systems often fail because chunking, indexing, and filtering are handled as afterthoughts. This skill gives practical patterns for semantic chunks, incremental updates, Qdrant filters, and context expansion.
actual_capabilities: Defines a semantic chunking approach based on Markdown section boundaries and overlapping chunks.
Shows file hash and content hash patterns for incremental re-indexing.
Outlines Qdrant collection setup with payload indexes for filtered retrieval.
Provides filter builder patterns for tenant, module, chapter, hardware tier, and proficiency filters.
Explains context expansion with previous and next chunk links.
Includes a small verification script for required skill files.
limitations: Does not provide a complete runnable RAG application.
Requires users to add authentication, authorization, and path validation for production APIs.
Assumes Qdrant and OpenAI-compatible embedding workflows.
Does not include evaluation datasets or automated retrieval quality tests.

use_cases.title: Create a Document Ingestion Pipeline
use_cases.description: Design chunking, hashing, embedding, and Qdrant upload steps for a structured documentation corpus.

use_cases.title: Improve Filtered Retrieval
use_cases.description: Add tenant, module, chapter, hardware tier, and proficiency filters to a vector search workflow.

use_cases.title: Plan Production RAG Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the examples to compare ingestion, indexing, retrieval, and context expansion design choices.

prompt_templates.title: Start a RAG Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a RAG ingestion pipeline for Markdown documents using semantic chunking, file hashes, embeddings, and Qdrant.

prompt_templates.title: Add Incremental Indexing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my document indexing flow and adapt it to use file hashes, content hashes, deleted file detection, and batched upserts.

prompt_templates.title: Build Filtered Retrieval
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Qdrant filter strategy for tenant isolation, content location filters, personalization fields, and parent document retrieval.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production RAG Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this RAG architecture for chunk quality, index freshness, payload indexes, context expansion, security controls, and operational risks.

output_examples.input: Plan an ingestion pipeline for a Markdown course library.
output: A staged ingestion design with crawler, parser, semantic chunker, embedder, and Qdrant uploader.
A change detection strategy using file hashes and deleted file reconciliation.
A list of required payload fields for filtering and context expansion.

output_examples.input: Improve retrieval for a multi-tenant educational assistant.
output: A filter plan that enforces tenant isolation before optional content filters.
Recommendations for payload indexes that support fast filtered search.
A context expansion approach that retrieves neighboring chunks in order.

output_examples.input: Review a draft RAG service before deployment.
output: A risk checklist covering path validation, webhook verification, authentication, and secret handling.
Operational recommendations for batching, incremental updates, and retrieval quality measurement.

best_practices: Use semantic boundaries and modest overlap instead of fixed character chunks.
Index every payload field that appears in frequent Qdrant filters.
Validate document roots, tenant identifiers, webhook signatures, and API access before production use.
anti_patterns: Re-indexing the full corpus on every document change.
Copying the API snippets without authentication or path allowlisting.
Running filtered search on unindexed payload fields at production scale.

faq.question: Does this skill include a complete RAG application?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation patterns and code snippets that users adapt to their own application.

faq.question: Which vector database does it target?
faq.answer: The examples focus on Qdrant collections, payload indexes, filters, scrolling, and point operations.

faq.question: Does it require OpenAI embeddings?
faq.answer: The examples use OpenAI embedding clients, but the architecture can be adapted to other embedding providers.

faq.question: Can it handle incremental document updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows file hash tracking, content hash chunk IDs, deleted file detection, and batched upserts.

faq.question: Is it safe to copy directly into production?
faq.answer: No. Add authentication, authorization, path validation, webhook verification, monitoring, and error handling first.

faq.question: Does it cover retrieval quality evaluation?
faq.answer: Only briefly. It lists target metrics, but it does not include evaluation datasets or automated scoring workflows.
